user.title: BirdWeather Setup
user.description: Pick a nearby public BirdWeather station, or type a name to search. Found {count} station(s). You can also paste a station ID directly.

data_description.search: Leave the station empty and submit to search by name instead of by location.
data_description.station_id: Choose a station, or paste its numeric station ID.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the BirdWeather API. Please try again.
error.invalid_station: No public station was found with that ID.
error.no_selection: Choose a station from the list, paste a station ID, or enter a search term.

abort.already_configured: This BirdWeather station is already configured

init.title: BirdWeather options
init.description: Tune the “notable species” ranking, the unusual-visitor alert, the confidence filters, and the species you want to be alerted about.

data_description.notable_rarity_weight: 100% = pure rarity; 0% = pure recency. Default 70% leans toward rarity with a small recency nudge.
data_description.absence_days: How long a known species must have gone unheard before it counts as an unusual visitor. Default 30 days.
data_description.feed_min_confidence: Suppress low-confidence “maybe” detections from the recent/last/notable/new sensors and the cards. 0% = show everything. The 24-hour total and diversity counts come straight from BirdWeather and aren’t affected.
data_description.alert_min_confidence: Don’t fire the new-species, unusual-visitor, or watched-species triggers below this confidence. 0% = alert on any. Independent of the hide filter, so you can see maybes but only be pinged on confident hits.
data_description.audio_enabled: Beta. Off by default. Adds a “play the call” button on the cards that plays a detection’s soundscape in the browser. Heads-up: if your BirdWeather station has audio sharing turned off, its soundscapes are silent — the button will appear but play nothing (we stream BirdWeather’s clip directly and can’t detect a silent one). Off means no audio_url is exposed, so no button.
data_description.watched_species: Pick from species your station has already detected. A “watched species detected” device trigger fires when one is heard.
data_description.watched_species_extra: Add species you haven’t recorded yet (e.g. a hoped-for visitor) — one common name per line, exactly as BirdWeather spells it.
